title: Swiggy Delivery Hub BFS Levels
description: Swiggy's central kitchen at node 0 needs to push menu updates to every delivery hub. The delivery network is given as an adjacency list `graph` of `n` nodes (0-indexed). Return the BFS traversal order starting from node 0. Within a BFS level, neighbors are visited in the order they appear in the adjacency list.

examples.explanation: From 0 we visit neighbours 1,2; then their neighbour 3.

constraints: Graph is undirected and may be disconnected — only return the component containing 0
hints: Queue + visited set ka classic BFS likh.
Adjacency list ka order preserve karna hai — bina sort kiye push kar.
Start node 0 ko visited mark karke push kar.

title: Razorpay Merchant Network DFS Order
description: Razorpay's fraud team wants to walk the merchant trust graph (undirected, adjacency list) from a given start node using DFS. Return the iterative DFS pre-order, visiting neighbours in the order they appear in the adjacency list (left to right).

examples.explanation: DFS from 0: go to 1, then 3, backtrack, then 2.

hints: Stack se iterative DFS likh — recursion bhi chalega per stack overflow se bach.
Stack me reverse order me push kar taaki pop pehle wala neighbour pehle de.
Visited set must — undirected graph me cycle hoga.

title: Indian Railways Connected Components
description: Indian Railways wants to know how many disconnected route clusters their station network has. Given `n` (number of stations) and `edges` (undirected route pairs), return the number of connected components.
hints: Adjacency list bana, fir har unvisited node se DFS/BFS.
Har naya DFS-launch ek component badhata hai.
Edges undirected hain — dono direction me push.

title: Flipkart Warehouse Cycle Detection
description: Flipkart's warehouse conveyor map should be a forest (no loops) for safety. Given `n` warehouses and undirected `edges`, return `true` if any cycle exists, else `false`.
hints: Union-Find: edge add karte waqt agar dono ends ka root same hai → cycle.
DFS bhi chalega: visited me jaate waqt parent ko exclude kar.
Multigraph (duplicate edge) bhi cycle counts.

title: Mumbai Local Train Shortest Stops
description: Mumbai local train network is unweighted (each adjacent station = 1 stop). Given an adjacency list `graph`, a `source`, and a `target`, return the minimum number of stops between them, or `-1` if unreachable.
hints: BFS layer count = unweighted shortest path.
Same-node case = 0.
Disconnect → -1.

title: Number of Land Islands
description: Given a 2D grid of '1' (land) and '0' (water), count the number of islands. An island is a maximal 4-directionally connected region of '1's. Input is `number[][]` (1 = land, 0 = water).
hints: Har 1 mile to DFS/BFS se poora island flood-fill kar de.
Counter +1 har naya island launch pe.
4 directions: up/down/left/right.

title: Max Island Area
description: Given a 2D `grid` of 0s and 1s, return the area (cell count) of the largest 4-connected island. If no land exists, return 0.
hints: DFS area count return karta jaye.
Visited mark, accumulate 1 + sum(neighbours).
Track global max across all DFS launches.

title: Surrounded Regions Flip
description: Given a 2D `board` of 0s (blank) and 1s (captured marker), flip every 1-region that is fully surrounded by 0s on all sides to 0. A region touching any border is NOT flipped. Return the new board.
hints: Border-touching 1s safe hain.
Border 1s se DFS karke unhe '2' mark kar — baaki 1s flip ho jayenge.
Final pass: 1→0, 2→1.

title: Rotting Oranges Multi-Source BFS
description: Each cell of the `grid` is 0 (empty), 1 (fresh orange), or 2 (rotten). Every minute, any fresh orange 4-adjacent to a rotten one rots. Return the minimum minutes until no fresh orange remains, or -1 if impossible.
hints: Multi-source BFS: saari rotten cells initially queue me daal.
Layer count = minutes elapsed.
Last me fresh remaining > 0 → -1.

title: Walls and Gates Distance
description: Each cell is `-1` (wall), `0` (gate), or `2147483647` (empty room). Fill every empty room with the distance to its nearest gate. If a room cannot reach any gate, leave it as is. Move 4-directionally.
hints: Multi-source BFS from all gates.
INF (2147483647) ko fill karte jao distance se.
Walls skip kar.

title: Course Schedule Feasibility
description: Given `numCourses` and prerequisite pairs `[a, b]` meaning `b` must come before `a`, return `true` if you can finish all courses, else `false`. Equivalent to: does the directed graph have no cycle?
hints: Topological sort feasible? → no cycle.
Kahn's BFS: degree-0 enqueue, decrement neighbours.
Processed count === numCourses → true.

title: Course Schedule Order Lex Smallest
description: Same as Course Schedule Feasibility but return a valid completion order (list of course IDs). If multiple are valid, return the lexicographically smallest one (treat as integer sequence). If impossible, return `[]`.
hints: Kahn's BFS, but use a min-heap (priority queue) of zero-indegree nodes for lex-smallest output.
Cycle ho gaya toh empty array.
JS me array + sort har push pe ya simple repeated min-find chalega.

title: Clone Undirected Graph (Adj List)
description: An undirected graph is given as an adjacency list `graph` of `n` nodes (0-indexed). Return a deep copy as a new adjacency list. The clone must be structurally identical: each `clone[i]` lists the same neighbours in the same order. (Since we use index serialization in/out, this reduces to producing a fresh array of arrays with identical content.)
hints: Naya array banao with same length, har neighbour list ko slice() kar.
Reference share nahi karna — independent inner arrays.
Empty graph empty return.

title: Word Ladder Shortest Length
description: Given `beginWord`, `endWord`, and a `wordList`, return the length of the shortest transformation sequence from begin to end such that each step changes exactly one letter and every intermediate word is in the list. Return 0 if impossible. Counted length includes both endpoints.
hints: BFS on words. Neighbours = words differing in exactly one letter.
Wildcard pattern bucket (h*t) speeds up adjacency lookup.
endWord wordList me na ho → 0.

title: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
description: Given an `m x n` grid of heights, water can flow from a cell to a 4-adjacent cell of equal or lower height. The Pacific touches the top and left edges; the Atlantic touches the bottom and right edges. Return the list of `[r, c]` coordinates from which water can reach BOTH oceans. Output sorted by `r` then `c`.
hints: Reverse flow trick: oceans se uphill DFS chala — visited cells that ocean reach.
Pacific aur Atlantic ke liye alag visited matrix.
Intersection of both = answer.

title: Keys and Rooms
description: There are `n` rooms 0..n-1. Room 0 is open initially; every other room is locked. `rooms[i]` is the list of keys you find in room i (each key opens one room). Return `true` if you can visit every room.
hints: Reachability problem — DFS/BFS from room 0.
Visited count === n → true.
Self-loops aur duplicate keys harmless.

title: Redundant Connection (Union-Find)
description: A tree on `n` nodes (1..n) had ONE extra edge added. Given the resulting `edges` (in input order), return the edge that, if removed, makes it a tree again. If multiple choices are valid, return the one that appears LAST in `edges`.
hints: Union-Find: process edges left-to-right.
First edge whose endpoints already share a root → that's the redundant one (and it's automatically the last one needed for cycle).
n nodes, n edges → exactly one cycle.

title: Number of Provinces
description: `isConnected[i][j] = 1` means city `i` and city `j` are directly connected (n x n symmetric matrix, diagonal is 1). A province is a maximal group of directly or indirectly connected cities. Return the number of provinces.
hints: Adjacency matrix → connected components problem.
DFS/BFS from every unvisited city; count launches.
Diagonal 1 ko ignore kar sakte (self loop).

title: Find Center of Star Graph
description: A star graph on `n` nodes (1..n) has exactly one center connected to every other node. Given exactly `n - 1` `edges`, return the center node.
hints: Center hi har edge me appear hota hai.
Pehle do edges check kar — common node = center.
O(1) algo possible hai.

title: Min Cost to Connect All Points (Prim's)
description: Given `points` (each `[x, y]`), connect all of them so any two are reachable. Edge cost between two points is Manhattan distance `|x1-x2| + |y1-y2|`. Return the minimum total cost (MST weight).
hints: MST problem; complete weighted graph.
Prim's with min-distance array — O(n^2) sufficient.
Single point → cost 0.

title: Bipartite Graph Check
description: Given an undirected `graph` (adjacency list), determine whether it can be 2-colored — i.e., is it bipartite? Graph may be disconnected.
hints: BFS/DFS color karte ja — neighbour ka opposite color.
Mismatch mile to false.
Multiple components handle kar.

title: Detect Cycle in Directed Graph
description: Given `n` and directed `edges` (each `[u, v]` meaning u → v), return `true` if a cycle exists.
hints: DFS with three states: WHITE, GRAY (in stack), BLACK (done).
GRAY node revisit → cycle.
Or: Kahn's processed != n → cycle.

title: List All Connected Components
description: Given `n` and undirected `edges`, return all connected components. Each component's vertices must be sorted ascending; components themselves must be sorted by their first (smallest) vertex.
hints: BFS/DFS sweep over unvisited nodes.
Each component: collect, sort, push.
Final list also sorted by first element.

title: Shortest Path in Binary Matrix
description: Given an `n x n` 0/1 grid, return the length of the shortest path from cell `(0,0)` to `(n-1, n-1)` traversing only 0-cells. You can move 8-directionally (including diagonals). Each visited cell counts (the path length is the number of cells). Return -1 if no path.
hints: BFS — 8 directions.
Start or end blocked → -1.
Visited matrix to avoid re-enqueue.

title: Island Perimeter
description: Given a `grid` containing exactly one island (connected 4-directionally) of 1s in a sea of 0s, return its perimeter — the number of edges shared between a land cell and either water or the grid boundary.
hints: Har land cell ka contribution = 4 - (land neighbours).
Ya har land cell ke 4 sides me check — out-of-bounds ya 0 → +1.
Pure scan pattern, BFS optional.

title: Flood Fill Color
description: Given an `image` matrix, a starting pixel `(sr, sc)`, and a `color`, replace the color of the connected (4-directionally) region of same-colored pixels starting at `(sr, sc)` with `color`. Return the modified image.
hints: BFS/DFS from (sr, sc).
Original color save kar — replace karte waqt confusion na ho.
Same target color → no-op.

title: Validate Graph Is a Tree
description: Given `n` and undirected `edges`, return `true` iff the graph is a tree: connected and acyclic. Equivalently: connected + has exactly `n - 1` edges.
hints: Edge count != n-1 → false fast.
Else BFS/DFS — visit count == n → true.
Union-Find bhi clean lagta hai.

title: Count Closed Islands of Water
description: Given a grid where 0 = water and 1 = land, return the number of CLOSED water-islands — i.e., 4-connected groups of 0s that do NOT touch any boundary.
hints: Border-touching 0 → not closed; flood-fill those out first (mark as 1).
Then count remaining 0-islands.
Standard surrounded-regions trick reused.

title: Ola Cab Reachable Stops
description: Ola wants to know all unique stops reachable from a `start` stop in a directed graph. Given `n` and `edges` (each `[u, v]` directed), and `start`, return the sorted list of reachable stops INCLUDING `start`.
hints: BFS/DFS from start, only forward edges.
Self always included.
Final list ko sort kar.

title: Knight Minimum Moves on 8x8 Board
description: On a standard 8x8 chessboard with rows/cols 0..7, a knight starts at `(sr, sc)` and wants to reach `(tr, tc)`. Return the minimum number of knight moves needed.
hints: BFS with the 8 knight offsets.
Same start/target → 0.
8x8 chhota grid — visited matrix.

title: Open The Lock (BFS on States)
description: A lock has 4 wheels each showing 0..9; it starts at "0000". Each move turns one wheel forward or backward by one digit (with wrap). Given a list of `deadends` (states that lock the dial) and a `target`, return the minimum number of moves to reach `target`, or -1.
hints: Implicit graph: each node is a 4-digit string, 8 neighbours (4 wheels × 2 directions).
BFS layer count.
Deadend including "0000" → -1 instantly.

title: As Far From Land As Possible
description: Given `n x n` grid with 0 (water) and 1 (land), return the largest Manhattan distance from a water cell to its nearest land cell. If no land or no water, return -1.
hints: Multi-source BFS from all land cells.
Last popped layer = answer.
All-land or all-water → -1.

title: Eulerian Path Existence (Undirected)
description: Given `n` nodes and undirected `edges` (multigraph allowed), return `true` iff the graph has an Eulerian path. An Eulerian path exists iff: (a) all edges lie in one connected component (isolated nodes ignored), and (b) the count of vertices with odd degree is 0 or 2.
hints: Degree count nikaal — odd-degree count check.
Connectivity: start from any vertex with edges, BFS, sab non-zero-degree nodes touched?
Empty edges → trivially true.

title: Shortest Bridge Between Two Islands
description: A grid has exactly two islands of 1s. Return the smallest number of 0-cells we must flip to 1 to connect them (4-directional connectivity). Equivalently, the minimum Manhattan-grid hop count between the two islands minus 1.
hints: Step 1: DFS to find one island, mark cells as 2 and add to queue.
Step 2: BFS outward from queue; the first time you hit a 1 = answer (layer-1).
Two-phase pattern very common.

title: Course Prerequisite Queries
description: Given `numCourses`, a list of direct prerequisite pairs `prerequisites[i] = [u, v]` meaning u must be taken before v, and a list of `queries[i] = [a, b]` asking 'is a a (direct or indirect) prerequisite of b?', return a boolean array answering each query. Assume the prerequisite graph is a DAG.
hints: Floyd-Warshall–style transitive closure: reach[i][j] true if path i→j.
Initialize edges; then for k mid relax: reach[i][j] = reach[i][j] || (reach[i][k] && reach[k][j]).
Each query O(1) lookup.
